when_to_help: Student confuses variable assignment with comparison
Student tries to mix types in math operations
Student is stuck on type conversion

key_concepts.explanation: Use '=' to store a value. 'name = Alice' creates a variable named 'name' that holds the value 'Alice'.
Python has str (text), int (whole numbers), float (decimals), bool (True/False). Use type() to check.
Convert with int(), float(), str(). Only works when values are compatible.

pro_tips: Variable names are case-sensitive: name != Name
print() can handle mixed types: print('Score:', score)
